/* Overview */ Technology is ONE PART of forces of production and, maybe, the one most likely to improve. But technology is not the SAME THING as forces of production, which is what i.e. says. But technology makes a good example to use for an EXAMPLE of a force of production liable to improve over time. Right?

It's limited to imply Marxism as merely a "method". It is a method as part of broader canon.

Reverted 1 edit by [[Special:Contributions/MaxWM7096|MaxWM7096]] ([[User talk:MaxWM7096|talk]]): Rv, discuss first

There is no such thing as a left-wing method. Marxism is not a political program, though it is typically used to prescribe one, and therefore has influence and implications that go beyond leftism. The listed sources are referring to Marxism in a very narrow and doctrinal sense which contradicts the introduction of this article's approach.
